metadata.seed: I would like to work on my avoidance behavior. I actually have an opportunity which I was looking for from a very long time to give a presentation. And now that I have this opportunity in front of me, I'm trying to avoid it.

patient.query: Yeah, it’s kind of overwhelming. On one hand, I really want to show I can do it, to prove to myself that I’m capable. But then, I start thinking about all the ways it could go wrong. Like, what if I mess up in front of everyone? I just feel this knot in my stomach, you know? It's like I'm just waiting for that moment when I’ll feel exposed or judged.

triggers: thinking about ways it could go wrong
waiting to feel exposed or judged
automatic_thoughts: what if I mess up in front of everyone?

entailment: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

patient.query: Yeah, it’s like a movie playing in my head. I picture everyone’s faces, their disappointment, and it just freezes me. I start thinking they’ll think I’m not good enough, or worse, that I’m a failure. It’s like I can’t shake that feeling, and then I get stuck. Instead of feeling excited or motivated, I just want to hide.

patient.query: Yeah, it really is tough. It feels like I can’t move or do anything. I start avoiding situations where I might have to face that fear. I know it’s not helping me in the long run, but the thought of stepping out there and risking that judgment... it just feels easier to play it safe. It's frustrating because I want to change, but then I just get caught up in that spiral.

triggers: situations where I might face fear

patient.query: When I think about it, my mind just races with all the negative stuff. I start imagining the worst—like what if I trip over my words or forget what I was going to say? I can almost hear those whispers, like people saying, “See? I knew they’d mess up.” It just feels so heavy, like I can’t breathe. It makes me want to retreat even more, just so I don’t have to feel that embarrassment.

automatic_thoughts: what if I trip over my words
what if I forget what I was going to say
people saying, 'See? I knew they'd mess up.'
